title: Dr. Yashwant Kumar Mishra vs The State Of Madhya Pradesh on 4 September, 2025
doc:
Dr. Yashwant Kumar Mishra vs The State Of Madhya Pradesh on 4 September, 2025

                                                                         1


                                   IN THE HIGH COURT OF MADHYA PRADESH
                                                AT JABALPUR
                                                                   BEFORE
                                             HON'BLE SHRI JUSTICE VIVEK JAIN

                                                  WRIT PETITION No. 35532 of 2024

                                                   DR. YASHWANT KUMAR MISHRA
                                                                      Versus
                                        THE STATE OF MADHYA PRADESH AND OTHERS
                           -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
                          Appearance:
                                  Shri L.C. Patne - Advocate (through V.C.) with Shri Abhay Pandey -
                          Advocate for the petitioner.
                                  Shri Ankit Agrawal - Advocate for intervenor.
                                  Shri Brian D'Silva- Sr. Advocate with Shri Harjas Singh Chhabra,
                          Advocate and Shri Rishi Shrivastava, Advocate for the respondent [R-3].
                                  Shri Swapnil Ganguly - Dy. A.G. for the respondents / State.
                           -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

                                                                      WITH

                                                 WRIT PETITION No. 35533 of 2024

                                              DR.(MRS.) MANJU SHARMA
                                                        Versus
                                     THE STATE OF MADHYA PRADESH AND OTHERS

                           -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
                          Appearance:
                                  Shri L.C. Patne - Advocate (through V.C.) with Shri Abhay Pandey -
                          Advocate for the petitioner.
                                  Shri Rishi Shrivastava - Advocate with Shri Harjas Singh Chhabra -
                          Advocate for respondent No.3.
                                  Shri Swapnil Ganguly - Dy. A.G. for the respondents / State.
                           -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

                                                                   ORDER

(Reserved on 28.07.2025) (Pronounced on 04.09.2025) Signature Not Verified Signed by: NAVEEN KUMAR SARATHE Signing time: 9/6/2025 2:14:08 PM 2 Both the petitions, raising identical grounds and on similar facts, are being decided by this common order. For the sake of convenience, facts and documents are referred from WP 35532/2024.

2. By way of this petition, the petitioner has put to challenge the order (Annexure P-4) whereby the respondent No.3 has been posted as In-charge Principal in Government Arts and Science PG College, Ratlam.

3. It is the contention of the petitioner that the petitioner is senior to respondent No.3 and is posted as Professor in Chemistry in the said college and by ignoring the candidature of petitioner and despite the petitioner also taking part in the process for posting in Prime Minister College of Excellence ('PMCE' for short), the respondent No.3 has been posted. It is contended that the petitioner attained 86.99 points in the assessment sheet prepared by the respondents while respondent No.3 attained 93.33 points in the assessment sheet. In WP 35533/2024, the petitioner therein has secured 76.53 marks, whereas the respondent No.3 therein also has secured marks higher than the writ petitioner.

4. The submission of counsel for the petitioner is manifold. It is contended that the respondents have erroneously adopted a wrong criteria for assessment inasmuch as they have given marks for ACR criteria whereas as per Rule 7(9) of M.P. Public Service (Promotion) Rules, 2002 (for short 'Rules of 2002') a benchmark was required to be fixed and all those teachers who crossed that benchmark, then their seniority was to be the criteria for such posting. However, in the present case the merit has been considered without any upper limit and it is contended that even in cases of promotions on the basis of merit-cum-seniority, then a benchmark merit has to be set up and all those who cross the benchmark then their seniority is reckoned. However, in the present case marks have been Signature Not Verified Signed by: NAVEEN KUMAR SARATHE Signing time: 9/6/2025 2:14:08 PM 3 granted for each year ACR in the consideration period and those who have better ACRs were given higher marks though the standard should have been to lay down particular benchmark for the same.

5. It is further contended by learned counsel for the petitioner that initially the scheme dated 08.07.2024 was framed which was followed by scheme dated 05.08.2024 which are placed on record as Annexure P-1 and P-2 respectively. It is contended that the petitioner had given two options and as per Clause 8 of the scheme dated 05.08.2024 and though the choices were made by the petitioner in the application form as per Clause-8, he could not get posting in the two colleges opted by him, but then the respondents should have carried out counseling so that lower meritorious teachers were not posted in any college. In other words, the grievance of the petitioner is that since he could not get any of the two colleges opted by him, he has not been allotted any college at all though persons lower than merit even as per the assessment carried out by the respondents have got a college for being posted thereat.

6. It is contended that either the number of choices should have been greater than two, as the number of Colleges was 72, or after exhausting the choices, the process for counselling was to be adopted so that a higher meritorious person should also have got a chance to be posted in a college of his choice. By pointing out to assessment sheet, it is contended that lesser scorers have been offered posting as Principals in PMCEs whereas petitioner has not been given posting in any PMCE.

7. The learned counsel for the petitioner further contended that the respondent No.3 is junior to petitioner in the feeder cadre of Professor and therefore, he could not have been posted in the college in which the petitioner is posted and the seniority of the petitioner had to be respected.

Signature Not Verified Signed by: NAVEEN KUMAR SARATHE Signing time: 9/6/2025 2:14:08 PM 4

8. The learned counsel for the petitioner also argued that various teachers in the assessment sheet who have been given marks have not completed 10 years service as Professor and Assistant Professor as per Clause 3.3 of the policy dated 05.08.2024 which required the candidate to have experience of teaching in Government college for at least 10 years. However, various teachers have been selected for being posted as Principal who do not have 10 years teaching experience.

9. Various averments were made on the anvil that the petitioner has to be treated as senior to respondent No.7 because the petitioner was promoted to the post of Professor in the year 2011whereas respondent No.3 has been selected as direct Professor in the year, 2018 and therefore, from the date of placement in the cadre of Professor the petitioner is a senior professor to respondent No.3.

10. Per contra, learned counsel for the State has vehemently opposed the petition by contending that as per policy Annexure P-2 vide Clause-8 only two options were required to be given and the petitioner gave two options but looking to his merit, he could not get either of the two colleges sought by him and therefore, no error can be found in that. It is further contended that the policy Annexure P-2 only promotes merit and the process for calculation /assessment of merit points has been laid down in para-4. The petitioner having participated in the process with open eyes now cannot challenge the process. It is further contended that the very process was challenged before this Court by Association of Professors being WP No.20865/2024 (Prantiya Shasakiya Mahavidyalayeen Pradhyapak Sangh and Ors. Vs. State of M.P. & Ors.) which was decided on 27.08.20224 and this Court has upheld the said policy dated 08.07.2024 as modified on 05.08.2024. If the petitioner had any grievance with the terms and conditions and modalities as contained in the said policy then he could Signature Not Verified Signed by: NAVEEN KUMAR SARATHE Signing time: 9/6/2025 2:14:08 PM 5 have challenged the very policy. The policy was challenged by the Association and some teachers but these grounds were not taken and therefore, the petitioner having participated, now he has no right to challenge the terms and conditions of the policy after facing rejection/non- selection.

11. On the question of inter-se seniority between promotee and direct Professors also the counsel for the State has vehemently opposed the petition.

12. Heard.

13. So far as the ground raised by the petitioner that as per Rule 7(9) of M.P. Public Service (Promotion) Rules, 2002 a benchmark has to be setup even if the posting on higher post was being made on the basis of merit- cum-seniority then all those who crossed the benchmark were required to be considered for posting on higher post and benchmark cannot be said to be unlimited benchmark by ignoring seniority and given only credence to merit. This is being stated to be something unknown to service jurisprudence and the respondent-State having erred in doing so. However, in the opinion of this Court, the said ground does not hold good because this is not a process for regular promotion, but this is only a process for posting of teachers in upgraded colleges which have been upgraded by the Central Government under a scheme of the Central Government.

14. The State wanted to post best of its manpower in said colleges for which the State has chalked out a mechanism. The Association as well as certain Professors had challenged the mechanism laid down but did not challenge the assessment criteria. The petitioner participated in the process with open eyes and after realizing that he is not getting offer to be posted as Principal has now woken up and challenged the assessment criteria. In the Signature Not Verified Signed by: NAVEEN KUMAR SARATHE Signing time: 9/6/2025 2:14:08 PM 6 opinion of this Court, the petitioner is estopped from challenging the assessment criteria which was disclosed to the petitioner well in time and he participated with open eyes. The criteria cannot be said to be in violation of any fundamental rights so as to rescue the petitioner out of estoppel to challenge the conditions of advertisement after rejection/non-selection. See: - (2017) 4 SCC 357 (Ashok Kumar v. State of Bihar)- (Para 13 to 18) (2002) 6 SCC 127 (Chandra Prakash Tiwari v. Shakuntala Shukla)- Para 32 to 34 (1986) Supp SCC 285 (Om Prakash Shukla v. Akhilesh Kumar Shukla)- Para 24

15. So far as the ground taken in the matter of inter-se seniory between the petitioner and respondent No.3 and the respondent No.3 claiming to be senior to the petitioner being a direct Professor, the said issue though was vehemently raised, but does not directly arise for consideration in the present petition for the reason that the petitioner also participated in the process for posting as Principal in a PMCE and now after facing non- selection has raised the ground of be being senior to respondent No.3. The petitioner has been promoted as Professor in the year, 2011 while the respondent No.3 has been directly appointed in the year 2018. This Court has passed a detailed order today in WP No.15013/2013 in terms of which the petitioner having been promoted in the year 2011 would be reckoned to be senior to respondent No.3. In WP 35533/2024, the writ petitioner has been promoted as Professor in the year 2006, while the respondent No.3 was directly selected as such in the year 2012. This writ petitioner is also, therefore, senior to the respondent No.3. Therefore, the respondents are required to either post a person who is substantively senior to the petitioner in the cadre of Professor or to post the petitioner at some other place, even if the petitioner is deemed to have not been selected for being posted in PMCEs.

Signature Not Verified Signed by: NAVEEN KUMAR SARATHE Signing time: 9/6/2025 2:14:08 PM 7

16. So far as the ground raised regarding various persons not completing 10 years of service before being selected for being posted as Principals in PMCEs is concerned, the State has contested the said ground taken by the petitioner that experience as Professor or Assistant Professor alone is not required but teaching experience of 10 years is required which can be on any position. The petitioner has not placed any particulars of the candidates mentioned in para-5.10 of the petition apart from appending a chart in para- 5.10, not supported by any document. Therefore, this Court is unable to cause any indulgence on the basis of chart appended in para 5.10 of the petition.

17. So far as the ground raised regarding no counseling of process being carried out, in the opinion of this Court, this ground has some substance. As per para-8 the following provision was made.

"8. दे श के 55 धानमं ी कॉलेज ऑफ ए सीलस एवं शेष 13 शासक य वशासी महा व ालय म ाचाय/ भार ाचाय या शै णक पद हे तु ऑनलाइन या से आवेदन आमं त कये गये है , जसक िलंक उ च िश ा वभाग क वेबसाइट https://highereducation.mp.gov.in पर उपल ध है । आवेदक को सा ा कार म उप थत होने पर TA/DA िनयमानुसार संबिं धत महा व ालय ारा दाय कया जाएगा। 
                                      आवेदक को पा तानुसार            येक पद पर अिधकतम 02                  थान पर
                                      वक प भरने का          ावधान दया गया है । आवेदन क अंितम ितिथ
                                      15.08.2024 तक बढ़ाई जाती है ।"
18. The aforesaid para-8 has to be read along with para-4 which mentioned as under:-
"4. पदांकन हे तु या: दे श के 55 धानमं ी कॉलेज ऑफ ए सीलस एवं 13 शासक य वशासी महा व ालय म ाचाय / भार ाचाय / ा यापको/सह ा यापको/सहायक ा यापक का पदांकन Signature Not Verified Signed by: NAVEEN KUMAR SARATHE Signing time: 9/6/2025 2:14:08 PM 8 पर ण सिमित के मा यम से, ावी य सूची के आधार पर कया जाएगा।"

19. Therefore, the respondents once were carrying out process for posting of Principals in as many as 73 colleges on merit basis, they were either required to take 73 options from each candidate or were required to carry out open counseling in absence of which merit cannot be ensured in the present process. This can be understood by an example that a person having S.No.4 in the merit may have opted for two such colleges which are taken by S.No.1 & 2 in the merit list. If the two colleges opted by the person at S.No.4 are taken by the persons at S.No.1 and 2 in the merit then the person at S.No.4 in the merit will not get any chance to be posted anywhere and the entire selection process to his extent will be reduced to nullity and a person much lower, say at Sr. No. 20 in the merit may get a College to be posted as Principal, while the person at Sr. No.4 will be left out. This type of action taken by the State defeats the intention of para-4 of the policy which is to promote merit and to post the Professors on merit. The State never mentioned in para-8 of the policy (Annexure P-2) dated 05.08.2024 that after taking two options no further counseling shall be carried out and it was a legitimate expectation of the candidates that if the two options failed, then open counseling will be carried out.

20. The manner in which two options are taken and if the concerned teacher does not get either of the two options then he would be nowhere in the merit something which totally defeats merits. Counselling could have been dispensed with only if those many options have been taken as were the number of colleges. A person working at Ratlam as Professor might have opted for the top colleges of the State situated at Indore, Bhopal, Gwalior, Jabalpur, etc. However, once he failed to get those colleges then the said person could have got any other nearby college or good college of Signature Not Verified Signed by: NAVEEN KUMAR SARATHE Signing time: 9/6/2025 2:14:08 PM 9 the State, say at Ujjain or Dewas, for which he not even got chance to exercise option and no counselling was conducted. Therefore, the interpretation of para-8 of the policy (Annexure P-2) defeats the intention of para-4 of the policy and therefore, it cannot be given stamp of approval by this Court.

21. The counsel for the State argued that this ground also cannot be taken as estoppel shall apply against the petitioner in this matter also. However, for this issue, estoppel shall not apply because this Court has reached to conclusion that interpretation of para-8 of the policy defeats the intention of para-4 thereof, which is to promote merit. Even para-8 does not bar counseling, and once a process for selection in colleges numbering as many as 72 was being carried out, then taking only two options, that too, prior to even the results being announced, cannot be the final mode of allocation. It is settled that merit can be ensured only if common counseling takes place to allocate appropriate seat to the candidate. The State has made mockery of the entire process by taking only two options for allocation to 72 posting places, and not even carrying out common counseling. If the candidate failed to secure the requisite posting place as per the only two choices that could have been opted by him, then dropping him from the process is highly illegal and illogical action on part of the State, that was considering posting to as many as 72 Colleges. It is very surprising, that such an unmindful process has been adopted by Higher Education Department of the State, that how preference for as many as 72 colleges can be operated by taking only 2 options, and not conducting any counseling thereafter.

22. Therefore, it is in the interest of justice that the State Government carries out open/common counseling or provides a chance to the Professors who participated in the selection process, to give as many options as are the Signature Not Verified Signed by: NAVEEN KUMAR SARATHE Signing time: 9/6/2025 2:14:08 PM 10 number of colleges, so that the intention of the State to protect merit and to post persons as per merit can be saved. Therefore, these petitions are disposed off with the following directions:-

(i) The petitioner shall either be allowed to exercise as many options as are the number of colleges, or the State would be required to conduct an common/open counseling for posting the Professors who have opted for being posted as Principals in PMCEs. Let this exercise be completed within 30 days.
(ii) If the petitioner does not get college of his choice and refuses posting in the college he gets then respondents should either post the petitioner to some other college or should post a person substantively senior to the petitioner as Principal in the college wherein he is presently working.
(iii) All other grounds raised in the petition are discarded.

23. No order as to costs.





                                                                                  (VIVEK JAIN)
                          nks                                                         JUDGE




Signature Not Verified
Signed by: NAVEEN KUMAR
SARATHE
Signing time: 9/6/2025
